# Sky Auto Player

> Sky Auto Player is a free, open-source Windows tool that plays music sheets in Sky: Children of the Light by simulating keyboard input through the public Windows SendInput API.

Sky Auto Player reads JSON, skysheet, or JSON-compatible TXT song files exported from the Sky Music web editor (specy/skyMusic) and plays them hands-free on Windows 10/11. It uses only the public Windows `SendInput` API for input simulation - no game memory access, no injection, no hooks, no anti-cheat bypass. Portable: no installer, no admin rights, no registry entries. Licensed under GNU GPL v3.0.

## Who it is for

PC players of *Sky: Children of the Light* who want to perform music sheets in-game without manually mashing keys. Useful for long songs, fast tempo passages, dense chords, and accessibility cases where manual input is impractical.

- [Landing page (English)](https://pumni.github.io/Sky-Auto-Player/)
- [Landing page (Tieng Viet)](https://pumni.github.io/Sky-Auto-Player/vi/)

- [Latest release download](https://github.com/pumni/Sky-Auto-Player/releases/latest)
- [Issue tracker](https://github.com/pumni/Sky-Auto-Player/issues)

## At a glance

- Operating system: Windows 10 and Windows 11 (64-bit)
- License: GNU GPL v3.0
- Language: Python 3.14 (free-threaded build)
- Input mechanism: Windows `SendInput` only - no game tampering
- Sheet formats: JSON, skysheet, JSON-compatible TXT
- Source sheet editor: specy/skyMusic (https://specy.github.io/skyMusic/)
- Source code: https://github.com/pumni/Sky-Auto-Player
- Download: https://github.com/pumni/Sky-Auto-Player/releases/latest

## Safety and ethics

Sky Auto Player performs no game memory access, no DLL injection, no process hooking, no debugger attach, no anti-cheat evasion, and no game file modification. It only sends standard keyboard input through the public Windows `SendInput` API - the same channel any keyboard macro uses. Automated music playback may still conflict with Thatgamecompany's Terms of Service; use the tool responsibly and at your own risk.
